title: ubin2uf
text: ubin2uf Copy binary data to a basic parameter or entity field. long ubin2uf (UHACT hOpr , int seqNr , UBYTE* pData , long sData ); Parameters hOpr The handle of an operation or entity seqNr The sequence number of the parameter or field (sequence numbers start with 1) pData The binary data to be copied sData The size of the data in pData Return Values For more information, see the "Call-In API Return Codes" topic. Use Library: ucall Description ubin2uf copies binary data to a basic parameter or entity field. You would typically use this function with a parameter or field of type raw or image . If the target has another datatype, conversion errors are likely to occur. ubin2uf converts the data from external format to Uniface format. If you have data that is already in Uniface format, use uprmputs rather than ubin2uf . Example: 3GL copy functions The following examples demonstrate the use of the functions ubin2uf , udbl2uf , ulong2uf , uuf2bin , uuf2dbl , and uuf2long . The service TESTSVC contains two operations: GETP which has three output parameters of types numeric , float , and raw , and SETP which has three input parameters of types numeric , float , and raw . These two services are defined as follows: Explain this code operation getp params numeric pnum : out; float pflt : out; raw praw : out; endparams pnum = 147483648 pflt = 3.1415926535897 fileload/raw "d:\temp\db2cli.gif", praw end operation setp params numeric pnum : in; float pflt : in; raw praw : in; endparams putmess "pnum = %%pnum" putmess "pflt = %%pflt" putmess "praw = %%praw" end Copy The following 3GL code will activate GETP, retrieve the three values, change them a bit, then pass them to SETP, which prints them to the message frame. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> test_params () { UHACT hEnv, hIn; /* Environment and instance handle */ UHACT hOp1, hOp2; /* Operation handles */ long actStatus, procErr; /* Activation status and procerr */ UBYTE* pBin; /* Pointer to binary parameter data */ long binLen; long longVal; double dblVal; ueopen(&hEnv); /* Get environment handle */ uinstnew (hEnv, (UCHAR *)"TESTSVC", 0, 0, UDEFAULT_COMM_MODE, 0, &hIn, &procErr); /* Instantiate TESTSVC */ uinstopr (hIn, (UCHAR *)"GETP", 0, &Op1);/* Instantiate operation GETP*/ uinstopr (hIn, (UCHAR *)"SETP", 0, &Op2);/* Instantiate operation SETP*/ uopract (hOp1, &actStatus, &procErr);/* Activate operation GETP */ uuf2long(hOp1, 1, &longVal); /* Get long value */ uuf2dbl (hOp1, 2, &dblVal); /* Get double value */ uuf2bin (hOp1, 3, &pBin, &binLen); /* Get pointer to binary data*/ ulong2uf(hOp2, 1, longVal / 2); /* Put long value */ udbl2uf (hOp2, 2, dblVal / 2); /* Put double value */ ubin2uf (hOp2, 3, pBin, 20); /* Put 20 bytes of binary data*/ uopract (hOp2, &actStatus, &procErr);/* Activate operation GETP */ uinstdel (hIn, 0); /* Destroy instance */ unifree (hOp1, pBin); /* Free the binary data */ ufreeh (&Op1); /* Free GETP operation handle*/ ufreeh (&Op2); /* Free SETP operation handle*/ ufreeh (&hIn); /* Free instance handle */ ufreeh (&hEnv); /* Free environment handle */ } Copy Here is what is SETP prints to the message frame: Explain this code pnum = 73741824 pflt = 1.57079632679485e+000 praw = GIF89a|R{$q{#|i{!{!{!{!{!{F{!{!{! Copy Related Links uuf2bin Return a pointer to the binary data in a basic parameter or entity field. Call-In API Return Codes Codes that can possibly be returned by functions from the 3GL call-in API are consistent with the values returned by the ProcScript function $procerror, unless noted otherwise.
